title_en: Author Biography and Modern Encyclopedic Tafsir
title_id: Biografi Penulis dan Tafsir Ensiklopedis Modern
content_id:
Muhammad at-Tahir ibn Muhammad ibn Muhammad at-Tahir ibn Asyur lahir pada 1296 H (1879 M) di Tunis dalam salah satu keluarga ulama paling terkemuka di Maghreb. Silsilah keluarganya berasal dari elit ulama Andalusia yang bermigrasi ke Afrika Utara setelah jatuhnya kekuasaan Muslim di Spanyol. Ia mendapat pendidikan yang luar biasa di Universitas Masjid Zaytuna di Tunis, salah satu lembaga pendidikan Islam tertua dan paling bergengsi di dunia Muslim, di mana ia akhirnya menjadi Rektornya dan Mufti Agung Tunisia.

Karier intelektual Ibnu Asyur mencakup transisi dari dunia keilmuan Islam tradisional ke era modern, dan ia terlibat secara aktif dengan pertanyaan-pertanyaan reformasi hukum Islam, kajian Al-Qur'an, dan hubungan antara tradisi Islam dan modernitas. Al-Maqasid al-Syariyyah al-Islamiyyah (Tujuan-Tujuan Hukum Islam) miliknya menjadi salah satu kontribusi modern terpenting untuk studi tujuan-tujuan hukum Islam (maqasid al-syariah), dan ia tetap menjadi referensi sentral dalam teori hukum Islam kontemporer.

Komentar Al-Qur'annya, Tafsir at-Tahrir wat-Tanwir (Pembebasan Makna dan Pencerahan Akal), adalah karyanya yang paling ekstensif, mencakup tiga puluh jilid. Karya ini disusun selama beberapa dekade kariernya dan merupakan puncak dari keterlibatannya dengan Al-Qur'an sebagai sarjana, guru, ahli hukum, dan pemikir. Judul mencerminkan ambisi gandanya: pembebasan (tahrir) dari interpretasi-interpretasi yang tidak memadai atau keliru dan pencerahan (tanwir) akal melalui pemahaman yang akurat tentang firman ilahi.

Tafsir at-Tahrir wat-Tanwir dibedakan oleh kombinasinya antara keilmuan eksegesis tradisional dengan perspektif historis, linguistik, dan ilmu sosial modern. Ibnu Asyur membawa pengetahuannya yang sangat luas tentang tafsir klasik, linguistik Arab, yurisprudensi Islam, dan usul al-fikih ke hadapan teks sambil juga mengacu pada kajian modern dan menangani perhatian-perhatian Muslim kontemporer.

Ia wafat di Tunis pada 1393 H (1973 M), meninggalkan sebuah tafsir yang semakin diakui sebagai salah satu pencapaian ilmiah Islam paling signifikan dari abad kedua puluh.
content_en:
Muhammad at-Tahir ibn Muhammad ibn Muhammad at-Tahir ibn Ashur was born in 1296 AH (1879 CE) in Tunis into one of the most distinguished scholarly families of the Maghreb. His family lineage traced to the Andalusian scholarly elite who had migrated to North Africa after the fall of Muslim rule in Spain. He received an exceptional education at the Zaytuna Mosque-University in Tunis, one of the oldest and most prestigious Islamic educational institutions in the Muslim world, where he eventually became its Rector and Grand Mufti of Tunisia.

Ibn Ashur's intellectual career spanned the transition from the traditional Islamic scholarly world into the modern era, and he engaged actively with questions of Islamic law reform, Quranic studies, and the relationship between Islamic tradition and modernity. His Al-Maqasid ash-Shari'yyah al-Islamiyyah (The Objectives of Islamic Law) became one of the most important modern contributions to the study of the purposes of Islamic law (maqasid ash-Shariah), and it remains a central reference in contemporary Islamic legal theory.

His Quran commentary, Tafsir at-Tahrir wat-Tanwir (The Liberation of Meaning and the Illumination of the Intellect), is his most extensive work, running to thirty volumes. It was composed over many decades of his career and represents the culmination of his engagement with the Quran as scholar, teacher, jurist, and thinker. The title reflects his dual ambition: liberation (tahrir) from inadequate or mistaken interpretations and illumination (tanwir) of the intellect through accurate understanding of the divine word.

Tafsir at-Tahrir wat-Tanwir is distinguished by its combination of traditional exegetical scholarship with modern historical, linguistic, and social scientific perspectives. Ibn Ashur brought his immense knowledge of classical tafsir, Arabic linguistics, Islamic jurisprudence, and usul al-fiqh to bear on the text while also drawing on modern scholarship and addressing contemporary Muslim concerns.

He died in Tunis in 1393 AH (1973 CE), leaving behind a tafsir that is increasingly recognized as one of the most significant Islamic scholarly achievements of the twentieth century.
